pr.title: Fix various literals and frame sizes
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Bulk decomp-matching cleanup generated by two scripts: one to find/fix incorrect literals and one to adjust stack frame sizes by adding, removing, or relocating padding. The author ran the scripts over all nonmatching functions and kept only changes that improved total match score, acknowledging a few false positives. The PR touched 84 files across fighter, game mode, stage, menu/interface, item, library, toy, and baselib code. decomp.dev reported +212 matched code bytes, +16 matched data bytes, 2 new matches, and 192 unmatched-item improvements, with one .sdata2 regression in gmresult.

change_classification.primary_type: decomp-matching bulk literal and stack-frame fix

what_changed: Added, removed, resized, or repositioned many stack-padding declarations, mostly using PAD_STACK(n) and sometimes explicit local arrays such as u8 _padA[16], u8 _padA[24], or u8 _padA[8].

Changed literal constants in nonmatching code where the new values improved match score, e.g. camera pause offsets in Camera_8002F9E4 changed from 85.0f/730.0f/35.0f scale to 5.0f/20.0f/3.0f scale in the excerpt.
Rewrote at least one boolean-return expression in ftCo_800A2718 from integer-style 0/1 returns and a combined condition into explicit false/true returns with nested control flow that matched better.
Corrected a facing-direction literal in ftColl_80078C70 from 1.0F to -1.0f in the excerpt.
Adjusted text/result display numeric literals, including HSD_SisLib_803A7548 scale/value in gmresult from 0.09F to 0.11f.
Converted or normalized some padding values, e.g. gmstaffroll PAD_STACK(0x60) became PAD_STACK(88), and several functions had PAD_STACK sizes changed from prior values.
The automated report recorded 2 new matches: main/melee/if/if_2F72 .sdata2 and main/melee/gr/granime grAnime_801C83D0.

The automated report recorded 192 improvements in unmatched items, with examples in ftCo_0A01, grbigblue .sdata2, grMuteCity_801F1A34, gmstaffroll fn_801AB200, mnsnap fn_802545C4, itmasterhandlaser it_802F063C, particle hsd_8039254C, lbcollision functions, lb_00F9, lbbgflash, lbaudio_ax, mplib, ftcoll, ftCo_Guard, ftCo_DownBound, ftmaterial, Kirby SpecialN, gmresult, gmresultplayer, gmclassic, gmallstar, and many stage files.
smart_moves: The author used scripts for broad search/repair but filtered results by actual total match-score improvement instead of blindly committing every candidate.
The PR separated two high-yield sources of mismatch noise: incorrect numeric literals and stack-frame size/padding drift.
The diff preserves local, minimal edits: many functions only gained or lost one PAD_STACK or explicit pad array, limiting semantic risk while improving codegen.

The approach produced measurable match gains across many unrelated subsystems, showing that systematic mechanical scans can recover scattered one-byte or few-byte improvements that are hard to prioritize manually.
Using explicit local pad arrays such as u8 _padA[...] in some functions suggests the author was willing to affect local variable layout/order when plain PAD_STACK placement was not enough.
decomp_lessons: Incorrect-looking gameplay or display literals may be decomp artifacts rather than intended source values; verify by match score and assembly, especially when a small numeric change affects .sdata2 or instruction selection.

Stack frame matching is sensitive not just to total padding size but also to where padding is declared relative to locals; moving PAD_STACK to an explicit local array before other locals can change frame layout.
Boolean expressions that are logically equivalent can compile differently; explicit true/false returns and nested if structure may match better than compact !A && !B forms.
Do not assume padding changes are final. A reviewer explicitly warned that some stack-frame changes may need to be reverted when the affected functions are fully matched.
Bulk automated decomp edits should be gated by objective match-score deltas and reviewed for false positives; the PR body says some false positives existed.
Small literal fixes can improve both code and data matching, including .sdata2 pool layout, but can also introduce local data regressions, as the bot reported for gmresult .sdata2.
naming_conventions: Temporary explicit padding locals used the pattern _padA with unsigned-byte arrays, e.g. u8 _padA[16], u8 _padA[24], and u8 _padA[8].
Existing project padding macro style PAD_STACK(n) was retained for most stack-frame adjustments.
The diff uses explicit bool literals false and true in ftCo_800A2718 rather than returning integer 0/1 from a bool function.
No new durable symbol-name convention was introduced; most touched functions retained address-derived or existing names such as ftCo_800A2718, fn_80184138, grAnime_801C83D0, and Camera_8002F9E4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Script candidate literal replacements over all nonmatching functions, then keep only changes that improve total match score.
Script stack-frame corrections by adding/removing padding and by changing PAD_STACK sizes.
If PAD_STACK at the end of declarations does not match, try an explicit u8 pad array before locals to influence local stack offsets.
Try equivalent control-flow rewrites for bool-returning functions; ftCo_800A2718 improved by replacing a combined condition with nested if/return true/false structure.
Track both code and .sdata2 consequences of literal changes; this PR gained a full .sdata2 match for if_2F72 but regressed gmresult .sdata2 by 4 bytes according to the bot.
Accept many small one-byte improvements when they are evidence-backed; the bot listed numerous +1 byte improvements across unmatched items.
review_feedback: decomp.dev bot reported matched code 66.30% (+0.01%, +212 bytes) and matched data 38.71% (+0.00%, +16 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches and 192 improvements in unmatched items.
decomp.dev bot also reported 1 regression in an unmatched item: main/melee/gm/gmresult .sdata2, -4 bytes, 88.78% to 84.69%.

Reviewer ribbanya approved and wrote: "The literal changes are nice. I don't mind the stack frame changes, but I think there will be at least some instances where the final match will need to change it back to what it was before."
searchable_terms: Fix various literals and frame sizes
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that revert or adjust stack-frame padding introduced in PR2467.
Search for functions touched by PR2467 that later fully matched, especially ftCo_800A2718, Camera_8002F9E4, gmresult functions, and grAnime_801C83D0.
Investigate whether gmresult .sdata2 regression from the 0.11f literal was later resolved.
Look for the scripts mentioned by lukechampine or later tooling that automates literal and PAD_STACK candidate testing.
Search future reviews mentioning PAD_STACK false positives or final matches needing previous frame sizes restored.
